#200","user":{"login":"vpuri3","id":36345239,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM2MzQ1MjM5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/36345239?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3","html_url":"https://github.com/vpuri3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2023-06-08T21:37:35Z","updated_at":"2023-06-15T12:45:33Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/pulls/201","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/pull/201","diff_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/pull/201.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/pull/201.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"the changes in #200 introduced `batch` kwarg to functionoperator. The API was that with `batch = true`, the last dimension of `u` will be the batch dim. With `batch = false`, the ND input array will be treated as one "column vector". This caused a lot of size mismatch errors since the rest of SciMLOperators takes the second dimension to be the batch dimension as that is what makes sense for matrices. And cases with ND arrays were handled with reshaping to 2D arrays. The principle issue became that we had different measures of length: `length(u)` for FunctionOperator with `batch = false`, `size(u)[1:end] |> prod` for `FunctionOP` with `batch = true`, and `size(u, 2)` for the rest. For an ND array and an arbitrary wrapper, it would be hard to know which measure to use. An increased reliance on `reshape` also causes confusion that may lead to unsafe behavior. Eg. applying matrices to arrays of wrong sizes should result in errors but could be done with reshape.

With this PR, I am limiting FunctionOperator w. `batch  = true` to only accept 2D arrays where the second dimension is the batch dimension. This is consistent with the rest of the package. FunctionOperator w. `batch = false` is still able to work with ND arrays as before. Further, I am removing operator evaluation and caching methods for ND arrays to avoid confusion. The only operator that is able to work with ND arrays is `FunctionOperator` w. `batch = false`. The rest of the operators work only with `AbstractVecOrMat`s where (optionally) batches are stacked in the 2nd dimension.

### new batch API for FunctionOperator
`batch` - Boolean indicating if the input/output arrays comprise of batched column-vectors stacked in a matrix. If `true`, the input/output arrays must be `AbstractVecOrMat`s, and the length of the second dimension (the batch dimension) must be the same. The batch dimension is not involved in size computation. For example, with `batch = true`, and `size(output), size(input) = (M, K), (N, K)`, the `FunctionOperator` size is set to `(M, N)`. If `batch = false`, which is the default, the `input`/`output` arrays may of any size so long as `ndims(input) == ndims(output)`, and the `size` of `FunctionOperator` is set to `(length(input), length(output))`.

- [x] do size check before mat_mul for FunctionOperator. Other concrete types, ie MatrixOp, ScalarOp manage that already.
- [x] rm `multidim.jl`
- [x] all scalar types must accept `AbstractArray`
- [x] add FunctionOp test inside a tensorprod

This is a major update. Please tag `0.3.0` after merging.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/201/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/201/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/comments/1592977013","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/pull/201#issuecomment-1592977013","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/201","id":1592977013,"node_id":"IC_kwDOHZ-dpc5e8uZ1","user":{"login":"vpuri3","id":36345239,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM2MzQ1MjM5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/36345239?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3","html_url":"https://github.com/vpuri3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-06-15T12:45:33Z","updated_at":"2023-06-15T12:45:33Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"@ChrisRackauckas can you take a second look sometime soon? https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/196

@ChrisRackauckas what is the expected heuristic here? Currently this only checks if we can convert to `AbstractMatrix`. Not if that's efficient or preferable.

Does `isconcrete(L) == true` implies we can `getindex`/ `setindex!`? Do we want separate traits `can_setindex, can_getindex`?

## EDIT
We can simplify the workflow to just two functions

- `isconvertible` indicating if `L` can be converted (cheaply) to an `AbstractMatrix`. This is true for lazy combinations of ScalarOp, MatrixOp, but not for TensorProdOp, InvertedOp. For FunctionOp, the behaviour can be set with kwarg `isconvertible`.
- `concretize(L)` returns a concrete type for `L`. It is a no-op when `L` is already concrete.

workflow in downstream packages:
```julia
if need_concrete_mat
    if !isconvertible(L)
        throw(ArgumentError("method need concrete matrix, but $L cannot be converted to a concrete type." *
                            "Please initialize $L as an `AbstractMatrix`, or `Number`"))
    end
    L = concretize(L)
end

"""
A nice-to-have would be a variant of the in-place `FunctionOperator` that can take an accumulating "5-arg" function, i.e., a function with signature `f!(y, x, α, β)` representing `y = α * L * x + β * y`. This is not something LinearMaps has, but the more I think about it the more this seems like the right way to specify an in-place matrix-free linear operator.
"""

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159/events","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159","id":1620422289,"node_id":"I_kwDOHZ-dpc5gla6R","number":159,"title":"[urgent] Overload operator application for `ComposedOperator`","user":{"login":"vpuri3","id":36345239,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM2MzQ1MjM5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/36345239?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3","html_url":"https://github.com/vpuri3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2023-03-12T16:16:51Z","updated_at":"2023-05-05T17:28:32Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"For `ComposedOperator` the present `update_coeffs(L, u, p, t)` interface will update each `op in L.ops` with `u`, which is not it's input.

https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/blob/31275d4e2b2f3671fb4c2811656dd555c4e1f24a/src/interface.jl#L22-L27

For example, with

```julia
N = 4
A = DiagonalOperator(rand(N); update_func = (d, u, p, t) -> copy!(d, u)) # A * u == u .^2
B = DiagonalOperator(rand(N); update_func = (d, u, p, t) -> copy!(d, u)) # A * u == u .^2
C = DiagonalOperator(rand(N); update_func = (d, u, p, t) -> copy!(d, u)) # A * u == u .^2

L = A ∘ B ∘ C
u = 0.1 * ones(N)

v = L(u, nothing, 0.0)
```
```julia
julia> v = L(u, nothing, 0.0)
4-element Vector{Float64}:
 0.00010000000000000003
 0.00010000000000000003
 0.00010000000000000003
 0.00010000000000000003
```
In this case, what we want to get by composing three squaring operations is `u .^ 8`, but we will get `u .^ 4` because `A,B,C` will be updated by `u, u, u`, not `u, u^2, u^4` respectively. What we want is to update `C` with `u`, `B` with its input `C * u = u .^ 2`, and `A` with `B * C * u = u .^ 4`. The operator update procedure must necessarily be interlaced with multiplication.

```julia
function (L::ComposedOperator)(u, p, t)
    v = u
    for op in reverse(L.ops)
        update_coefficients!(op, v, p, t)
        v = op * v
    end
    v
end
```
So we need to overload update-coeffs and operator application for `ComposedOperator`. It would be wise to take a second look at other composite operators to ensure such a thing isn't happening anywhere else. Maybe we need to define `update_coeffs` overload for `AdjointOperator, TransposedOperator` to pass in `u'`. Should also take a second look at FunctionOperator.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/comments/1536564578","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159#issuecomment-1536564578","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159","id":1536564578,"node_id":"IC_kwDOHZ-dpc5blh1i","user":{"login":"vpuri3","id":36345239,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM2MzQ1MjM5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/36345239?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3","html_url":"https://github.com/vpuri3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-05-05T17:28:32Z","updated_at":"2023-05-05T17:28:32Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"TODO - preserve order of operations in scalaroperator constructor: ie A * λ vs λ * A and apply updatecoeffs accordingly. 76#issuecomment-1537584512","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/176","id":1537584512,"node_id":"IC_kwDOHZ-dpc5bpa2A","user":{"login":"vpuri3","id":36345239,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM2MzQ1MjM5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/36345239?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3","html_url":"https://github.com/vpuri3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-05-08T00:28:17Z","updated_at":"2023-05-08T00:28:17Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Objects like `MatrixOperator` which contain \"actual data\" need an in-place and an out-of-place update method. My initial thought was to pass an update function to `MatrixOperator` as in `L = MatrixOperator(A; update_func = A_update_func)` where `update_func` may have an in-place and an out-of-place method. However since the calling signature of both IIP and OOP is the same, `update_coeffs(L, u, p, t)`/ `update_coeffs!(L, u, p, t)` it would be impossible to distinguish between IIP and OOP methods.\r\n\r\nAnother solution is to ask for two separate functions: one IIP and one OOP. Eg. `MatrixOperator(A; update_func = oop_func, update_func! = iip_func)`. """
A nice-to-have would be a variant of the in-place `FunctionOperator` that can take an accumulating "5-arg" function, i.e., a function with signature `f!(y, x, α, β)` representing `y = α * L * x + β * y`. This is not something LinearMaps has, but the more I think about it the more this seems like the right way to specify an in-place matrix-free linear operator.
"""

@gaurav-arya , can you help me figure out why this MVP isn't working?

```julia
using OrdinaryDiffEq, LinearSolve, Test

f(u, p, t) = copy(u)
f(du, u, p, t) = copy!(du, u)

N = 4
u0 = zeros(N)
p = rand()
prob = ODEProblem(f, u0, (0.0, 1.0), p)

integ1 = init(prob, TRBDF2(autodiff = true))
integ2 = init(prob, TRBDF2(autodiff = false))

integ3 = init(prob, TRBDF2(linsolve = KrylovJL_GMRES(), autodiff = true))
integ4 = init(prob, TRBDF2(linsolve = KrylovJL_GMRES(), autodiff = false))

integ6 = init(prob, TRBDF2(linsolve = IterativeSolversJL_GMRES(), autodiff = false))
integ5 = init(prob, TRBDF2(linsolve = IterativeSolversJL_GMRES(), autodiff = true)) ##### FAILS

```

The error is emerging from a `mul!(Y::Vector{Float64}, W::OrdinaryDiffEq.WOperator{...}, B::Vector{Float64}` which is then passed down to `FunctionOperator` -> `SparseDiffTools` -> `UJacobianWrapper` -> `FunctionWrappersWrappers`. The same thing happens for other implicit time-steppers (KenCarp, Rosenbrock).

@gaurav-arya it looks like we'll have to deal with this error now as it is not related to `IterativeSolvers.jl`. Check the example below

```julia
using OrdinaryDiffEq, LinearSolve, Test

@testset "nojac.jl" begin
    f(u, p, t) = copy(u)
    f(du, u, p, t) = copy!(du, u)

    N = 4
    u0 = rand(N)
    p = rand()
    prob = ODEProblem(f, u0, (0.0, 1.0), p)

    for Alg in (TRBDF2, KenCarp47, Rosenbrock23, Rosenbrock32, Rodas4,)
        for ad in (true, false,)
            for Linsolve in (Nothing, KrylovJL_GMRES)
                @info "alg: $Alg, ad = $ad, linsolve = $Linsolve"

                sol =  solve(prob, Alg(autodiff = ad, linsolve = Linsolve()))
                @test sol.retcode == ReturnCode.Success
            end
        end
    end
end
```
```julia
julia> include("a.jl")                                                                                                    
[ Info: alg: TRBDF2, ad = true, linsolve = Nothing                                                                        
[ Info: alg: TRBDF2, ad = true, linsolve = KrylovJL_GMRES                                                                 
nojac.jl: Error During Test at /Users/vp/.julia/dev/OrdinaryDiffEq/a.jl:4                                                 
  Got exception outside of a @test                                                                                        
  No matching function wrapper was found!                                                                                 
  Stacktrace:                                                                                                             
    [1] _call(#unused#::Tuple{},
```

The stacktrace is similar to before: when `autodiff = true`, something goes off and we are unable to apply `mul!(::Vector{Float64}, ::WOperator, ::Vector{Float64})`.  Maybe JacVec isn't passing the arguments down correctly to `auto_jacvec!`...

Trivial example: `ldiv!` with diagonal is ~15x slower than `mul!`.
```julia
julia> n=1024;

julia> u=rand(n); v=copy(u); D=Diagonal(copy(u));

julia> @btime mul!($v,$D,$u);
  129.625 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)

julia> @btime ldiv!($v,$D,$u);
  2.030 μs (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
```
This will also be useful for passing `jac` to `SplitFunction`. Maybe this could help https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaCore.jl/issues/41 not sure.

I propose extending `InvertibleOperator` as follows:
```julia
struct InvertibleOperator
    A # fwd SciMLOperator
    F # inv
end
```
where `F` can be the inverse (SciML)Operator, or any object with 3 arg mul!, * {"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/180","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/180/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/180/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/180/events","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/180","id":1704621452,"node_id":"I_kwDOHZ-dpc5lmnWM","number":180,"title":"Cheap inverse of A+kI if cheap inverse from A","user":{"login":"gaurav-arya","id":43899491,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQzODk5NDkx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/43899491?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gaurav-arya","html_url":"https://github.com/gaurav-arya","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gaurav-arya/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gaurav-arya/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gaurav-arya/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gaurav-arya/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gaurav-arya/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gaurav-arya/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gaurav-arya/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gaurav-arya/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gaurav-arya/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2023-05-10T20:58:56Z","updated_at":"2023-05-10T21:21:43Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"If `A` has a cheap inverse, `A+kI` nearly always does too. It is very important to exploit this, e.g. for cheap inverses of the W-operator in implicit ODE solves: if the user provides a Jacobian with a cheap inverse, we don't want to waste it.

The solution isn't super clear, but some ideas:

- `MatrixOperator(A) + k*I` = `MatrixOperator(A + k*I)`, rather than `AddedOperator(...)`
-  Similarly, additions to identity should be specialized upon for other types, e.g. tensor product operators. The idea is to avoid a lazy addition when possible to avoid the need for Krylov solves.
-  When a user specifies `F = FunctionOperator(...)`, somehow we should make it that `F+kI` is also a `FunctionOperator` when possible, e.g. maybe the user specifies a general class of `op`s that work for arbitrary `k`. (This one is vague, and definitely needs design work, but hopefully we can find an elegant solution.)
- As a last resort, we can also consider things like the [Sherman-Morrison formula](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sherman%E2%80%93Morrison_formula) which apply using OrdinaryDiffEq, LinearSolve, LinearAlgebra
using SparseArrays, Symbolics, ModelingToolkit
using IncompleteLU, AlgebraicMultigrid, Sundials
using BenchmarkTools

const N = 32
const xyd_brusselator = range(0, stop = 1, length = N)
brusselator_f(x, y, t) = (((x - 0.3)^2 + (y - 0.6)^2) <= 0.1^2) * (t >= 1.1) * 5.0
limit(a, N) = a == N + 1 ? 1 : a == 0 ? N : a
function brusselator_2d_loop(du, u, p, t)
    u = reshape(u, N, N, 2)
    du = reshape(du, N, N, 2)

    A, B, alpha, dx = p
    alpha = alpha / dx^2
    @inbounds for I in CartesianIndices((N, N))
        i, j = Tuple(I)
        x, y = xyd_brusselator[I[1]], xyd_brusselator[I[2]]
        ip1, im1, jp1, jm1 = limit(i + 1, N), limit(i - 1, N), limit(j + 1, N),
                             limit(j - 1, N)
        du[i, j, 1] = alpha * (u[im1, j, 1] + u[ip1, j, 1] + u[i, jp1, 1] + u[i, jm1, 1] -
                       4u[i, j, 1]) +
                      B + u[i, j, 1]^2 * u[i, j, 2] - (A + 1) * u[i, j, 1] +
                      brusselator_f(x, y, t)
        du[i, j, 2] = alpha * (u[im1, j, 2] + u[ip1, j, 2] + u[i, jp1, 2] + u[i, jm1, 2] -
                       4u[i, j, 2]) +
                      A * u[i, j, 1] - u[i, j, 1]^2 * u[i, j, 2]
    end

    vec(du)
end
p = (3.4, 1.0, 10.0, step(xyd_brusselator))

function init_brusselator_2d(xyd)
    N = length(xyd)
    u = zeros(N, N, 2)
    for I in CartesianIndices((N, N))
        x = xyd[I[1]]
        y = xyd[I[2]]
        u[I, 1] = 22 * (y * (1 - y))^(3 / 2)
        u[I, 2] = 27 * (x * (1 - x))^(3 / 2)
    end
    u
end
u0 = init_brusselator_2d(xyd_brusselator) |> vec
prob_ode_brusselator_2d = ODEProblem(brusselator_2d_loop, u0, (0.0, 11.5), p)

@info "Vanilla Brusselator TRBDF2, KrylovJL"
@btime solve(prob_ode_brusselator_2d, TRBDF2(linsolve = KrylovJL_GMRES()), save_everystep = false);

@info "Vanilla Brusselator KenCarp47, KrylovJL"
@btime solve(prob_ode_brusselator_2d, KenCarp47(linsolve = KrylovJL_GMRES()), save_everystep = false);

@info "Vanilla Brusselator KenCarp47, LUFactorization"
@btime solve(prob_ode_brusselator_2d, TRBDF2(linsolve = LUFactorization()), save_everystep = false);

du0 = copy(u0)
jac_sparsity = Symbolics.jacobian_sparsity((du, u) -> brusselator_2d_loop(du, u, p, 0.0), du0, u0)
f = ODEFunction(brusselator_2d_loop; jac_prototype = float.(jac_sparsity))
prob_ode_brusselator_2d_sparse = ODEProblem(f, u0, (0.0, 11.5), p)

@info "Sparse Brusselator, TRBDF2, KrylovJL"
@btime solve(prob_ode_brusselator_2d_sparse, TRBDF2(linsolve = KrylovJL_GMRES()), save_everystep = false);

function incompletelu(W, du, u, p, t, newW, Plprev, Prprev, solverdata)
    if newW === nothing || newW
        Pl = ilu(convert(AbstractMatrix, W), τ = 50.0)
    else
        Pl = Plprev
    end
    Pl, nothing
end

# Required due to a bug in Krylov.jl: https://github.com/JuliaSmoothOptimizers/Krylov.jl/pull/477
Base.eltype(::IncompleteLU.ILUFactorization{Tv, Ti}) where {Tv, Ti} = Tv

@info "Sparse Brusselator, TRBDF2, KrylovJL, ILU preconditioner"
@btime solve(prob_ode_brusselator_2d_sparse,
             TRBDF2(linsolve = KrylovJL_GMRES(), precs = incompletelu,
                       concrete_jac = true), save_everystep = false);



function algebraicmultigrid(W, du, u, p, t, newW, Plprev, Prprev, solverdata)
    if newW === nothing || newW
        Pl = aspreconditioner(ruge_stuben(convert(AbstractMatrix, W)))
    else
        Pl = Plprev
    end
    Pl, nothing
end

@info "Sparse Brusselator, TRBDF2, KrylovJL, AMG preconditioner"
@btime solve(prob_ode_brusselator_2d_sparse,
             TRBDF2(linsolve = KrylovJL_GMRES(), precs = algebraicmultigrid,
                       concrete_jac = true), save_everystep = false);

function algebraicmultigrid2(W, du, u, p, t, newW, Plprev, Prprev, solverdata)
    if newW === nothing || newW
        A = convert(AbstractMatrix, W)
        Pl = AlgebraicMultigrid.aspreconditioner(AlgebraicMultigrid.ruge_stuben(A,
                                                                                presmoother = AlgebraicMultigrid.Jacobi(rand(size(A,
                                                                                                                                  1))),
                                                                                postsmoother = AlgebraicMultigrid.Jacobi(rand(size(A,
                                                                                                                                   1)))))
    else
        Pl = Plprev
    end
    Pl, nothing
end

@info "Sparse Brusselator, TRBDF2, KrylovJL, Jacobi preconditioner"
@btime solve(prob_ode_brusselator_2d_sparse,
             KenCarp47(linsolve = KrylovJL_GMRES(), precs = algebraicmultigrid2,
                       concrete_jac = true), save_everystep = false);


@info "Vanilla Brusselator, CVODE_BDF, LapackDense"
@btime solve(prob_ode_brusselator_2d, CVODE_BDF(linear_solver = :LapackDense), save_everystep = false);

@info "Vanilla Brusselator, CVODE_BDF, GMRES"
@btime solve(prob_ode_brusselator_2d, CVODE_BDF(linear_solver = :GMRES), save_everystep = false);

nothing

StaticWOperator regression

```julia
using OrdinaryDiffEq, StaticArrays, BenchmarkTools

function rober(u, p, t)
    y₁, y₂, y₃ = u
    k₁, k₂, k₃ = p
    dy₁ = -k₁ * y₁ + k₃ * y₂ * y₃
    dy₂ = k₁ * y₁ - k₂ * y₂^2 - k₃ * y₂ * y₃
    dy₃ = k₂ * y₂^2
    SA[dy₁, dy₂, dy₃]
end

prob = ODEProblem{false}(rober, SA[1.0, 0.0, 0.0], (0.0, 1e5), SA[0.04, 3e7, 1e4])

for Alg in (Rodas4, Rosenbrock23)
    @info "$Alg"
    int = init(prob, Alg())
    @assert int.cache.W isa OrdinaryDiffEq.StaticWOperator

    @btime solve(prob, $Alg(), save_everystep = false)
end
```

For developers of this library, this is what you should know about the new design:

- Operator constructors called by the user should accept `update_func` and `accepted_kwargs` arguments, and preprocess them using the `preprocess_update_func` utility before making the struct, as done here.

n = 256
l = 2π

dx = l / n

u  = @. sin(5x)cos(7x)  + im*cos(x) |> complex  
du = @. 5cos(5x)cos(7x) - 7sin(5x)sin(7x)  - im*sin(x) |> complex
ddu = @. -25sin(5x)cos(7x) - 35cos(5x)sin(7x) - 35cos(5x)sin(7x) - 49sin(5x)cos(7x) - im*cos(x) |> complex

k  = fftfreq(n, 2π*n/l) |> Array
m  = length(k)
tr = plan_fft(u)

Lf = FunctionOperator(
     (du,u,p,t) -> mul!(du, tr, u), u,u,
          isinplace=true,
          T=ComplexF64,
          op_adjoint = (du,u,p,t) -> ldiv!(du, tr, u),
          op_inverse = (du,u,p,t) -> ldiv!(du, tr, u),
          op_adjoint_inverse = (du,u,p,t) -> ldiv!(du, tr, u),
)

ik = im * DiagonalOperator(k)
∂x = Lf \ ik * Lf
∂xx= Lf \ -DiagonalOperator(k.^2) * Lf
Dx = cache_operator(∂x, u)
Dxx = cache_operator(∂xx, u)

@show ≈(Dx * u, du; atol=1e-8)
@show ≈(mul!(copy(u), Dx, u), du; atol=1e-8)
@show ≈(Dxx * u, ddu; atol=1e-8)
@show ≈(mul!(copy(u), Dxx, u), ddu; atol=1e-8)

## test

N = (u,_,_) -> -im*abs2.(u).*u

prob = SplitODEProblem(L,N,u0,(0,1.))
sol = solve(prob, ETDRK4(), dt=0.05, saveat=0.5);

They depend on `ExponentialUtilities.jl` that, upon a cursory look, requires full matrices.

I tried your problem with Tsit5 which doesn't require a full matrix, and it worked fine. In general, the fastest way to solve such a problem is with an IMEX timestepper and a krylov linear solver.

```julia
F = SplitFunction(L, N; jac_prototype = L)
prob = ODEProblem(F, u0, (0,1.))
sol = solve(prob, TRBDF2(autodiff = false, linsolve = KrylovJL_GMRES()))
```

However, that functionality is broken right now. Split operators won't work with implicit/semiimplicit solvers, but we're looking to fix that in the next 1-2 weeks.  Till then I'd recommend sticking with any of the stiff explicit solvers.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/comments/1565655444/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/125","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/125/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/125/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/125/events","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/125","id":1512825422,"node_id":"I_kwDOHZ-dpc5aK-JO","number":125,"title":"Propagate `kwargs` through `update_coefficients!`","user":{"login":"gaurav-arya","id":43899491,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQzODk5NDkx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/43899491?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gaurav-arya","html_url":"https://github.com/gaurav-arya","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gaurav-arya/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gaurav-arya/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gaurav-arya/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gaurav-arya/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gaurav-arya/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gaurav-arya/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gaurav-arya/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gaurav-arya/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gaurav-arya/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":14,"created_at":"2022-12-28T14:00:05Z","updated_at":"2023-05-29T18:55:49Z","closed_at":"2023-05-29T18:37:43Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"In some rare cases, an operator depends on more state than just `u`, `p`, and `t`. For example, the `W` operator in `OrdinaryDiffEq` depends on `dtgamma`. 

If `kwargs` could be propagated through `update_coefficients!`, we'd be able to take advantage of the recursive infrastructure in these cases too. The call might look something like `update_coefficients!(W, u, p, t; dtgamma)`: operators which do not use `dtgamma` would just ignore the extra info, while an operator which would like to use it catches it and uses it. Of course, if an operator requires `dtgamma` (or contains an operator that does), it could not be used as a regular `SciMLOperator` for the usual use cases, but it wouldn't make sense for that to be possible in any case if the operator needs extra state.

For this to work, it seems like all that would be needed to be done is:

- Recursively propagate `kwargs` in `update_coefficients!` for `ComposedOperator`'s, etc.
- User-provided `update_func` signatures which do not catch `kwargs` should be detected and modified to catch `kwargs` (and not use them), so that the usual use case of `update_coefficients!` still works seamlessly. (Something like https://stackoverflow.com/a/42579941?)

In general, something like this just seems like good design: while the positional args `u, p, t` capture 99% of use cases, there are definitely cases (like the `W` operator) which could naturally be expressed as a SciMLOperator if we just allowed an extra state dependence. Happy to take a stab at this if it's deemed a good idea.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/125/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/125/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/comments/1567426188","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/125#issuecomment-1567426188","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/125","id":1567426188,"node_id":"IC_kwDOHZ-dpc5dbQaM","user":{"login":"vpuri3","id":36345239,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM2MzQ1MjM5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/36345239?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3","html_url":"https://github.com/vpuri3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-05-29T18:55:49Z","updated_at":"2023-05-29T18:55:49Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"@ChrisRackauckas can you also bump version? thanks

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/LinearSolve.jl/issues/277","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/LinearSolve.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/LinearSolve.jl/issues/277/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/LinearSolve.jl/issues/277/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/LinearSolve.jl/issues/277/events","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/LinearSolve.jl/issues/277","id":1616014162,"node_id":"I_kwDOFsu4xM5gUmtS","number":277,"title":"Using HYPRE solver in DifferentialEquations.jl causes stack overflow","user":{"login":"termi-official","id":9196588,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjkxOTY1ODg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/9196588?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/termi-official","html_url":"https://github.com/termi-official","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/termi-official/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/termi-official/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/termi-official/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/termi-official/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/termi-official/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/termi-official/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/termi-official/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/termi-official/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/termi-official/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-03-08T22:03:58Z","updated_at":"2023-04-25T18:10:49Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Stack overflow at https://github.com/SciML/LinearSolve.jl/blob/main/src/common.jl#L160-L162

I think some dispatches are missing. E.g. I had to overload `LinearSolve.needs_concrete_A(::HYPREAlgorithm)` to come this far.

Reproducer
```julia
using DifferentialEquations, HYPRE, MPI, LinearSolve
MPI.Init()
HYPRE.Init()
function lorenz!(du,u,p,t)
    du[1] = 10.0*(u[2]-u[1])
    du[2] = u[1]*(28.0-u[3]) - u[2]
    du[3] = u[1]*u[2] - (8/3)*u[3]
end
u0 = [1.0;0.0;0.0]
T = 100.0
tspan = (0.0,T)
problem = ODEProblem(lorenz!,u0,tspan)
Δt_save = 0.1
LinearSolve.needs_concrete_A(::HYPREAlgorithm) = true
timestepper = ImplicitEuler(linsolve = HYPREAlgorithm(HYPRE.GMRES))
integrator = init(
    problem, timestepper,
    adaptive=true, abstol=1e-3, reltol=1e-3,
    progress=true, progress_steps=1,
    saveat=Δt_save, verbose=true
);
integrator = TimeChoiceIterator(integrator, 0.0:Δt_save:T)
for (u_uc,t) in integrator
    @show u_uc
end
```

Also, it is not clear how to pass a preconditioner (e.g. HYPRE.BoomerAMG) to the iterative solver (e.g. Hypre.GMRES) with the current interface. It doesn't seem easy to construct a diagonal operator as the default is a batched diagonal operator.

Also, is the below line dangerous? It looks like it would drop the `update_func`.
https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/blob/31275d4e2b2f3671fb4c2811656dd555c4e1f24a/src/matrix.jl#L116","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/158/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/158/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/comments/1464992627","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/158#issuecomment-1464992627","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/158","id":1464992627,"node_id":"IC_kwDOHZ-dpc5XUgNz","user":{"login":"vpuri3","id":36345239,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM2MzQ1MjM5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/36345239?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3","html_url":"https://github.com/vpuri3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-03-11T19:29:10Z","updated_at":"2023-03-11T19:29:10Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Yes there are two different use cases.

(1) `DiagonalOperator` can be used for the very common case where `L.A` is a diagonal matrix.
```julia
N, K = 3, 4
d = Diagonal(N)
u = rand(N, K)
A = DiagonalOperator(d) # MatrixOperator
v = A * u
```
will pointwise multiply each column on `u` with `d`.

(2) Consider the following case: you are evolving an ODE with `size(u) = (N,K)`, ie you have `K` trajectories of size `N`. A very common operation you want to perform is pointwise multiplication, i.e. for every trajectory `k`, you want to do `d[:,k] .* u[:,k]`. This is common when you want to form a nonlinear term like `u^2` in a Navier-Stokes or a Burgers solver. That's what `BatchedDiagonalOperator` allows you to do:
```julia
N, K = 3, 4
d = rand(N, K)
u = rand(N, K)
A = DiagonalOperator(d) # BatchedDiagonalOperator
v = A * u
```

If you look at the methods, `DiagonalOperator(d::AbstractArray)` is a convenience function that dispatches to `MatrixOperator` when `d isa AbstractVector`, and `BatchedDiagonalOperator` otherwise. We have tests to make sure the correct type is instantiated (`MatrixOperator`, or `BatchedDiagonalOperator`). Feel free to add more tests if you think there are corner cases. {"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DifferentialEquations.jl/issues/881","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DifferentialEquations.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DifferentialEquations.jl/issues/881/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DifferentialEquations.jl/issues/881/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DifferentialEquations.jl/issues/881/events","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/DifferentialEquations.jl/issues/881","id":1299845712,"node_id":"I_kwDOA3ziS85NehJQ","number":881,"title":"Defining a Parameter/Structures Interface","user":{"login":"ChrisRackauckas","id":1814174,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE4MTQxNzQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1814174?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas","html_url":"https://github.com/ChrisRackauckas","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ChrisRackauckas/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":11,"created_at":"2022-07-10T06:54:42Z","updated_at":"2023-03-12T15:40:27Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Guiding issues:

- https://github.com/SciML/ModelingToolkit.jl/issues/318
- https://github.com/SciML/ModelingToolkit.jl/issues/1088
- https://github.com/SciML/ModelingToolkit.jl/issues/1678
- https://github.com/SciML/NeuralPDE.jl/issues/533
- https://github.com/FluxML/Optimisers.jl/issues/95
- https://discourse.julialang.org/t/parameter-types-in-diffeqflux-jl-versus-differentialequations-jl/79635
- https://github.com/SciML/DiffEqBase.jl/pull/773

Guiding designs:

- https://github.com/FluxML/Functors.jl
- https://github.com/Metalenz/FlexibleFunctors.jl (https://discourse.julialang.org/t/ann-flexiblefunctors-jl-functors-with-per-instance-parameters/64935)
- https://github.com/rafaqz/ModelParameters.jl
- https://github.com/rafaqz/Flatten.jl
- https://github.com/invenia/ParameterHandling.jl
- https://github.com/SciML/RecursiveArrayTools.jl
- https://github.com/jonniedie/ComponentArrays.jl
- https://github.com/paschermayr/ModelWrappers.jl

For a long time we were able to get away with just "let `p` be any struct", though an edge case came up, and then a second one, and then a third one, etc. until it became clear we need to at least have an interface defined for "what can be done with `p`". Similarly, we have allowed a loose definition of `u0` as "anything that can broadcast effectively, and if required can do linear algebra", but this has become limiting.

The main issue really started cropping up with adjoint sensitivity analysis. In that case, one might want to extend the differential equation to include `p`, e.g. `[u0;p]` for `InterpolatingAdjoint`, which then means that `p` needs to be able to "act" like a `u0`. If that's the case, then if we assume `u0` needs to be able to broadcast and do linear algebra, suddenly this requirement is also on `p`. While this works for "most" `p`'s that people use in practice, it does impose a limitation where using say a tuple of arrays for parameters works for "standard" equation solving, but fails with adjoints. 

This then requires that we ask the question: what is "the" SciML parameters or structure interface? Currently it has been essentially `AbstractArray`, since `AbstractArray` is the only thing that effectively defines linear algebra, with extensions allowed with extra functionality is not used. However, it's probably more than overdue to both tighten and enlarge this interface by directly defining what it's actions should be.

If we do this right, then people should be able to use weird structures everywhere and have things "just work", or tell them which functions to define in order for things to just work. How do we get there?

## What should a SciML Parameters interface do?

1. It needs to be fast and type-stable. If it's not that, people with ignore it.
2. It needs to be overloadable. If someone's structure doesn't work, they should have a clear way to make it work.
3. It needs to be able to throw appropriate errors about missing structural information. For example, which function needs to be defined.
4. It needs to support standard Julia types out of the box.
5. It needs to have clearly defined behavior for restructuring and destructuring to/from arrays. In particular, restructuring should always use the values from the restructure vector, and not change it like the Optimisers.jl behavior on Complex (https://github.com/FluxML/Optimisers.jl/issues/95)
6. Restructuring/destructuring on the simplest types needs to be a no-op. This is required or else supporting calls to C/Fortran is almost impossible, which is required for many wrapper solver libraries (for example, supporting Sundials) but also even things as pervasive as supporting linear algebra.
7. It needs to support naming and named indexing. This would give ModelingToolkit a way to interpret code down to its DSL in a way that preserves naming, and generate structures that are more interpretable for users than its flat vectors.
8. It needs to be able to add tags that some things are "parameters" while other things are not. What things should optimize, what things are constant.
9. Non-SciMLStructure `p`'s still need to work in the solvers: e.g. only the places that need more control should check `isscimlstructure` (and if false, throw an error that this is required).

## Ideas from Other Libraries / Do we need a separate library for this? Can we use another one?

- This would be core enough to SciML that we would need to make sure we had admin rights all over it and the ability to hotfix etc. as necessary, so from that fact alone it would need to live in SciML. I think that's a no brainer.
-  There have been a number of factors for why it was not adopted more widely throughout SciML, the main reason being that restructure/destructure was undeniably broken until fairly recently, where it still has some unintuitive behaviors for the purpose of equation solvers (https://github.com/SciML/NeuralPDE.jl/issues/533, https://github.com/FluxML/Optimisers.jl/issues/95). However, the general idea of Functions.jl is a good starting place, and I think no matter what structure we settle on we should support Functor definitions at least as a subset of the interface. It (and nothing else) check all of the boxes, like having a hierarchical naming system for ModelingToolkit.
- https://github.com/Metalenz/FlexibleFunctors.jl might be a better starting point than Functors.jl (to the point where starting as a fork may be helpful). Its design is very simple and intuitive, but it's missing a lot, like potentially necesssary mapping operations, hierarchical naming, etc.
- https://github.com/rafaqz/ModelParameters.jl and https://github.com/jonniedie/ComponentArrays.jl come from a very different angle and support the hierarchical naming, but require very specific type wrappers, meaning they aren't "interfaces" but types. While their types are great (and a current good solution for using DiffEq), if we're going to modify the solvers we probably want to do it around interfaces so that "simple" and intuitive types that users gravitate towards (like arrays of arrays) are supported.

## Questions

### Do we extend the solvers to allow for a SciML Structure/Parameter for `u0` or always flatten?

This is potentially a considerable amount of work, but with a potentially considerable amount of gain. It would be an interesting solution to the adjoint sensitivity problem: if `[u0,p]` "just works" because arrays of arrays just work because arrays of arrays are supported in the structure interface, then some code becomes easier. But other things, like how to define mass matrices, become 😕. Maybe all matrices need to only be defined on the flattened form, and then LinearSolve.jl can do the restructure/destructure to make it all work nicely?

Supporting this in OrdinaryDiffEq.jl is probably not "too" hard. Every `2 * k1` would need to be like a `fmap(*,k1,2)`. It would probably be best to just define that as an operator `2 ⊗ k1` or something so the code is easier to read, but that would up the barrier to entry. For the broadcast operations, it would just require that we specialize `@..` effectively, which would probably be the hardest part (if `all(isSciMLStructure,vals)` -> special dispatches?). `@..` would no longer just be "Fast Broadcast" anymore though, since it would have a different definition/operation on things like arrays of arrays (but then would allow their support without requiring a RecursiveArrayTools.VectorOfArray wrapper, which groups like DynamicalSystems.jl have been asking for years).

For other solvers, we'd have to do destructure/restructure in the wrapper code so it just presents as arrays to the underlying solver. To not have a performance hit, we will need (6) to hold. 

I think we should do it, but it will be a challenge.

### Do we provide a tables interface for reading/writing to data?

I can see this as very valuable for people working with CSVs and the such. https://github.com/rafaqz/ModelParameters.jl showed it's possible. We are redesigning the operator interface to SciML.ai in https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl. This issue has come up in discussions on how to pass in parameters to function objects. The problem here, as I see it, is that there are two kinds of parameters we want to pass to functions/operators:

1. parameters we want to do automatic-differentiation/ adjoint calculation on
2. operator specific context or struct objects that contain mesh, numerical parameters, discretization information, etc.

The former can in most cases be cast as an `AbstractArray`, while the latter is almost always a complex data-structure. Currently `SciML` only permits passing in one parameter via the `p` positional argument in  `f(du, u, p, t)`. Problems arise when one has to do adjoint calculations with `p` when it is a complex data-structure. Obviously writing restructure, restructure methods for all the data-types in `p` can become cumbersome.

https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/pull/143 is solving this problem by allowing users pass the two types of parameters separately. The proposed design would allow you to pass array-like AD parameters in `p`, and function-dependent data-structures using keyword arguments:

```julia
    # Test scalar operator which expects keyword argument to update, modeled in the style of a DiffEq W-operator.
    γ = ScalarOperator(0.0; update_func=(args...; dtgamma) -> dtgamma, accepted_kwarg_fields=(:dtgamma,))

    dtgamma = rand()
    @test γ(u,p,t; dtgamma) ≈ dtgamma * u
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/DifferentialEquations.jl/issues/comments/1465230844/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159/events","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159","id":1620422289,"node_id":"I_kwDOHZ-dpc5gla6R","number":159,"title":"[urgent] Overload `update_coeffs` for `ComposedOperator`","user":{"login":"vpuri3","id":36345239,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM2MzQ1MjM5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/36345239?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3","html_url":"https://github.com/vpuri3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-03-12T16:16:51Z","updated_at":"2023-03-12T16:16:51Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"For `ComposedOperator` the present `update_coeffs(L, u, p, t)` interface will update each `op in L.ops` with `u`, which is not it's input.

https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/blob/31275d4e2b2f3671fb4c2811656dd555c4e1f24a/src/interface.jl#L22-L27

For example, with

```julia
N = 4
A = DiagonalOperator(rand(N); update_func = (d, u, p, t) -> u) # A * u == u .^2
B = DiagonalOperator(rand(N); update_func = (d, u, p, t) -> u)
C = DiagonalOperator(rand(N); update_func = (d, u, p, t) -> u)

L = A ∘ B ∘ C
u = rand(N)

v = L * u
```
In this case, what we want to get by composing three squaring operations is `u .^ 8`, but we will get `u .^ 4` because `A,B,C` will be updated by `u, u, u`, not `u, u^2, u^4`. What we want is to update `C` with `u`, `B` with its input `C * u = u .^ 2`, and `A` with `B * C * u = u .^ 4`. The operator update procedure must necessarily be interlaced with multiplication.

```julia
function (L::ComposedOperator)(u, p, t)
    v = u
    for op in reverse(L.ops)
        update_coefficients!(op, v, p, t)
        v = op * v
    end
    v
end
```
So we need to overload update-coeffs and operator application for `ComposedOperator`. It would be wise to take a second look at other composite operators to ensure such a thing isn't happening anywhere else. Maybe we need to define `update_coeffs` overload for `AdjointOperator, TransposedOperator` to pass in `u'`.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159/events","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159","id":1620422289,"node_id":"I_kwDOHZ-dpc5gla6R","number":159,"title":"[urgent] Overload `update_coeffs` for `ComposedOperator`","user":{"login":"vpuri3","id":36345239,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM2MzQ1MjM5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/36345239?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3","html_url":"https://github.com/vpuri3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2023-03-12T16:16:51Z","updated_at":"2023-03-12T17:30:35Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"For `ComposedOperator` the present `update_coeffs(L, u, p, t)` interface will update each `op in L.ops` with `u`, which is not it's input.

https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/blob/31275d4e2b2f3671fb4c2811656dd555c4e1f24a/src/interface.jl#L22-L27

For example, with

```julia
N = 4
A = DiagonalOperator(rand(N); update_func = (d, u, p, t) -> copy!(d, u)) # A * u == u .^2
B = DiagonalOperator(rand(N); update_func = (d, u, p, t) -> copy!(d, u)) # A * u == u .^2
C = DiagonalOperator(rand(N); update_func = (d, u, p, t) -> copy!(d, u)) # A * u == u .^2

L = A ∘ B ∘ C
u = 0.1 * ones(N)

v = L(u, nothing, 0.0)
```
```julia
julia> v = L(u, nothing, 0.0)
4-element Vector{Float64}:
 0.00010000000000000003
 0.00010000000000000003
 0.00010000000000000003
 0.00010000000000000003
```
In this case, what we want to get by composing three squaring operations is `u .^ 8`, but we will get `u .^ 4` because `A,B,C` will be updated by `u, u, u`, not `u, u^2, u^4` respectively. What we want is to update `C` with `u`, `B` with its input `C * u = u .^ 2`, and `A` with `B * C * u = u .^ 4`. The operator update procedure must necessarily be interlaced with multiplication.

```julia
function (L::ComposedOperator)(u, p, t)
    v = u
    for op in reverse(L.ops)
        update_coefficients!(op, v, p, t)
        v = op * v
    end
    v
end
```
So we need to overload update-coeffs and operator application for `ComposedOperator`. It would be wise to take a second look at other composite operators to ensure such a thing isn't happening anywhere else. Maybe we need to define `update_coeffs` overload for `AdjointOperator, TransposedOperator` to pass in `u'`. Should also take a second look at FunctionOperator.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/comments/1465254554","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159#issuecomment-1465254554","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/159","id":1465254554,"node_id":"IC_kwDOHZ-dpc5XVgKa","user":{"login":"vpuri3","id":36345239,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM2MzQ1MjM5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/36345239?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3","html_url":"https://github.com/vpuri3","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vpuri3/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-03-12T17:30:34Z","updated_at":"2023-03-12T17:30:34Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"It is a mistake for the simple reason that
```julia
julia> A(B(C(u, p, t), p, t), p, t) ≈ (A ∘ B ∘ C)(u, p, t)
false

julia> A(B(C(u, p, t), p, t), p, t)
4-element Vector{Float64}:
 1.0000000000000008e-8
 1.0000000000000008e-8
 1.0000000000000008e-8
 1.0000000000000008e-8

julia> (A ∘ B ∘ C)(u, p, t)
4-element Vector{Float64}:
 0.00010000000000000003
 0.00010000000000000003
 0.00010000000000000003
 0.00010000000000000003
```
This problem occurs specifically when you want to compose nonlinear operators, i.e `L` depends on `u`. One application of SciMLOperators is to be the `ODEFunction` passed to `OrdinaryDiffEq`. If you want `A` to be the operator that represents pointwise multiplication with the ODE solution, you would write it as follows:
```julia
N = 4

uODE = rand(N)

A = DiagonalOperator(rand(N); update_func = (d, u, p, t) -> copy!(d, uODE)) # A * u = uODE .* u
B = DiagonalOperator(rand(N); update_func = (d, u, p, t) -> copy!(d, uODE))
C = DiagonalOperator(rand(N); update_func = (d, u, p, t) -> copy!(d, uODE))

L = A ∘ B ∘ C
u = rand(N)

v = L(u, nothing, 0.0) # ≈ uODE .^3 .* u
```
In my applications till now, I haven't needed to compose multiple nonlinear operations. I have only needed to compose nonlinear operations with linear operations, thus I never came across this error. For example, to solve 1D Burgers, `du/dt = -u * du/dx - nu * d2u/d2x`. So my SciMLOp is
```julia
A = nu * D2x
C =  DiagonalOperator(u0, update_func = (v, u, p, t) -> copy!(v, u)) * Dx
dudt = A - C
```
So when I pass `dudt` to `OrdinaryDiffEq`, things work correctly as `update_func` correctly forms the nonlinear operation. Of course, I'd have to go back and fix that code later.

However, we should do things correctly. `L = A ∘ B ∘ C` means you apply `C`, then `B`, and then `A`, even if they are nonlinear. If we want to make an operator that multiplies (pointwise) with the current ODE solution, which is what you have in mind, we should write it using your `kwargs` approach.
```julia
uODE 
A = DiagonalOperator(zeros(N), update_func = (d, u, p, t; uODE) -> copy!(d, uODE)))
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/SciMLOperators.jl/issues/comments/1465254554/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
